assertions.text: Correctly identifies all licence types needed for the content and use described
Rights holder identification names the type of organisation to approach, not just 'the rights holder'
Key terms section covers territory, duration, platform, exclusivity, and fee basis
Grant of rights clause specifies what is licensed, for what purposes, on which platforms, in which territories, for what duration
Compensation section is present even if compensation is zero
Form is written in language a non-lawyer can understand
All relevant clearance categories are identified based on the content description
High-risk items are flagged explicitly with a risk note
Checklist distinguishes between clearances the producer can handle and those requiring legal counsel
Research steps are specific to the content type, not a generic search the internet list
Steps are ordered from fastest/cheapest to slowest/most resource-intensive
Orphan works possibility is addressed
All four key questions are answered: commercial use, modification, attribution, share-alike
A model attribution line is provided that meets the licence requirements
If a specific use case is described, a clear yes/no verdict is given
All six GDPR transparency requirements are addressed: controller identity, purpose, legal basis, recipients, retention, rights
Language is plain enough for a non-specialist participant to understand
The notice explicitly flags areas requiring legal review
Every source from the raw notes appears in the compiled bibliography
Citation format is consistent across all entries with no mixing of styles
Missing information is flagged with bracketed notes, not guessed or fabricated
